title: crepis glomerata
intro: Crepis glomerata, also known as Clustersed Hawksbeard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is often found growing in dry, rocky areas. Crepis glomerata has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and digestive problems. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for its numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health.
